# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

We want this project to be a practical, respectful place to work. We expect contributors, maintainers, and users to act in good faith and treat each other professionally.

## Expected Behavior

- Be direct and constructive.
- Focus feedback on code, behavior, and decisions.
- Assume good intent until evidence suggests otherwise.
- Keep discussion relevant to the project.

## Unacceptable Behavior

- Harassment, discrimination, intimidation, or personal attacks.
- Deliberate disruption of discussions or pull requests.
- Sharing private information without permission.
- Any conduct that makes participation unsafe or hostile.

## Enforcement

Project maintainers may remove or lock content, close issues or pull requests, or restrict participation when behavior crosses the line. Repeated or severe violations may lead to a permanent ban from project interaction.

## Reporting

If you need to report a conduct issue, open a private GitHub security advisory or contact the maintainers through the repository's existing project channels.
